query: Np Hard optimization problem in biology and health

t1.1.name: Energy Minimization Algorithms
t1.1.description: Computational methods for finding the global energy minimum in protein folding, involving complex search spaces and approximate solutions due to NP-hardness.

t1.name: Protein Structure Prediction and Folding
t1.description: Computational problems related to predicting three-dimensional protein structures from amino acid sequences, including protein folding pathways. These are classic NP-hard problems with significant implications for drug design and disease understanding.

t2.1.description: The computational challenges of aligning three or more biological sequences simultaneously, proven to be NP-complete with implications for phylogenetics and comparative genomics.

t2.description: NP-hard problems in reconstructing complete genomes from DNA fragments and aligning multiple genetic sequences, fundamental to genomics and personalized medicine.
